title: Is there a Japanese Blu ray with an English Sub Dub
display_title: Is there a Japanese Blu ray with an English Sub Dub
wikitext:
[]: Is there a Japanese Blu-ray with an English Sub/Dub??             Quote Post by NegaBanks » Wed Apr 10, 2024 11:52 pm I figured you geeks would be able to confirm this for me. Does there exist a Japanese Blu-ray that contains both English sub/dub and "fly me to the moon"? Certain sites like to combine reviews for different versions so there's probably some conflicting info. I haven't been able to find concrete answers. I want to buy a physical version of Eva, and the way I see it, there are only 2 good options for a gaijin like me to buy: the Platinum Collection (thin pack) or (hopefully) a Japanese Blu-ray containing all my needs.

[]: Re: Is there a Japanese Blu-ray with an English Sub/Dub??             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Thu Apr 11, 2024 4:22 pm Sorry to say, no. No Japanese releases have English subs or dubs. I know this off-hand, but here's some evidence. First of all, a very good resource for Eva home media is this site . It doesn't specify if a release has subs or what kind, but it does provide the very helpful cat. # for each release. So if you google the cat. # for the Japanese BD Box for instance (my search term was KIXA-90501 "subtitles"), you get immediate answers on several sites. Evangelion only exists on BD with eng. subs with Fly Me to the Moon removed, as released by G Kids, and the old dub was only released on their fanciest collector's edition.

[]: Re: Is there a Japanese Blu-ray with an English Sub/Dub??             Quote Post by NegaBanks » Mon Apr 22, 2024 10:49 pm Axx°N N. wrote: Sorry to say, no. No Japanese releases have English subs or dubs. I know this off-hand, but here's some evidence. First of all, a very good resource for Eva home media is this site . It doesn't specify if a release has subs or what kind, but it does provide the very helpful cat. # for each release. So if you google the cat. # for the Japanese BD Box for instance (my search term was KIXA-90501 "subtitles"), you get immediate answers on several sites. Evangelion only exists on BD with eng. subs with Fly Me to the Moon removed, as released by G Kids, and the old dub was only released on their fanciest collector's edition.

[]: Re: Is there a Japanese Blu-ray with an English Sub/Dub??             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Apr 23, 2024 1:12 pm NegaBanks wrote: Do you know what the Japanese Rebuilds are like? I know they don't have dubs, but I read on an Ebay listing that they do have subs.

[]: Re: Is there a Japanese Blu-ray with an English Sub/Dub??             Quote Post by NegaBanks » Tue Apr 23, 2024 1:44 pm Axx°N N. wrote: NegaBanks#943102 wrote: Do you know what the Japanese Rebuilds are like? I know they don't have dubs, but I read on an Ebay listing that they do have subs.

[]: Re: Is there a Japanese Blu-ray with an English Sub/Dub??             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Apr 23, 2024 2:16 pm NegaBanks wrote: One small problem that makes the English release inferior is that the 3.0 + 1.11 blu-ray has a color banding issue. The EvaGeeks YouTube channel has a great review of the 3.0 + 1.11 blu-rays. The visual quality took a hit to save space for 2 audio tracks. Not the case for the 4k blu-ray, but I don't have a 4k TV, so...
